sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.110 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Are there residually finite hereditarily just infinite groups that are [r]{a)} amenable but not solvable? [r]{b)} amenable but not elementary amenable? [r]{c)} of intermediate word growth? [r]{d)} of intermediate subgroup growth? All examples that we know are either linear (hence Tits Alternative applies), or have a quotient with property (T) (hence cannot be amenable). { M.\,Vannacci}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
